item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is rad as hell.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know it's old but I would really love to see an interview with the ref. I am imagining him having an "uncanny valley" like way of talking. If you stick to subjects that relate to sports or getting injuries to the hospital he seems human but if you go outside those areas he sounds like a chat bot encountering info it's not designed to discuss.

Edit to say +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Am I going to have to separate you two?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah… stick to Madden

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Some deliciously classic body horror. Nice.

Also, do not Google necrotising fasciitis. Just assume that you know what it is. You do not want to see any photos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't downvote, but I have to agree with MisterFlames. I really like this SCP with the backstory given here in the discussion, but on its own I don't feel that the article is very strong at all. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is why I don't do the sports.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SOOOOO WEIRDDDD i love it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry here, Drewbear, but I went against the flow and downvoted it.

I did like the spoiler once I read it, but this doesn't hit me as a particularly good SCP. My problem has to do with the disappearing ambulance, and there's just a certain amount of what-the-fuck that my disbelief just can't cross over. Considering the number of people in the Foundation that work on things like the ambulance, I would think that someone would… catch on.

So, there's my reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ambiguous as to whether or not it causes the original referee to be detained. I like it, but am not sure if that was intentional.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A. Yes. ;-)

B. So would I; I think this may be the first one that considers this. At least, I can't think of any others.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A. DW reference?

B. Awesome. I would actually love to see more skips out there exploring this idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Appropriately horrifying. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…its remains will dissolve into a slurry of undifferentiated human cellular tissue…

This combined with the missing cartilage (and the math there) are what really did it for me. +1

Nitpick: my cursory websearch shows that necrotizing fasciitis is caused by many types of bacteria, but only bacteria. I suggest changing "microorganisms" to "bacteria" for the sake of specificity.

e: for clarity

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Liked it in the forums, like it here. Well done, Drewbear.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
injured with potentially life-threatening injuries

This needs a little work. That said, it's a pretty creepy concept and I like how it's pulled off. Just some polish on the execution is really all this needs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like. It's like a physical trauma version of SCP-049. Upvote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Wall of text and numerous run-on-sentences couldn't keep me from upvoting this crazy thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. I love this even more, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
spoilers, sweetie
– hide block

Actually, I'm envisioning this as a case of 2 anomalous thingies cross-testing in the wild. I mean, given how many things are out there, it's kind of inevitable that some would encounter each other before the Foundation or a GOI came across them.

In this case, they were originally the injury-causing referee, who would just vanish in the crowd once players had been injured, and the ambulance, which would hybridize anyone inside the back when the sirens were running, with the secondary effect that people didn't notice the hybridization. The ambulance showed up at one of the referee's games and their properties resonated, making them into a larger, more dangerous SCP-level thingie. So now it's in its current form, PLUS people forget about the ambulance once it's delivered the victims. Notice that it completely drops out of the document after delivery, when the Foundation would certainly want to investigate it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They're sent upstate to live on a farm, where they can run free and chase rabbits all day long. Yes. That's certainly what happens.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is it wrong that I now want to write a tale about the aftermath of this thing showing up at a pro wrestling show?

Man, CM Cena vs. the Kanertaker would be the greatest thing ever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like what you did with the 5 words, and the concept itself is good enough for an upvote. This confused me, though:

an ambulance will arrive at the sporting venue within 4 minutes of the time of injury.

So is that a probability thing, or is the ambulance part of the effect?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What I really want to know is what happens to the hybrids who don't die…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
New SCP in response to Dmatix's 5-word challenge. Mine were: ambulance, bin, flesh, referee, reject.

Also, SCP-1737 variably self-identifies as "Mr. Reject", "Mr. Roget", "Mr. Corvette" or "Mr. Matix." Just so you know.
